content.full_text:
SACRAMENTO
770 L STREET, SUITE 8OO
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
TELEPHONE (9761 446-67s2
LAW OFFICES OF
NIELSEN, MERKSAMER,
HODGSON, PARRINELLO & MUELLER
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDINC PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS
650 CALIFORNIA STREET. SUITE 2650
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIfORNIA 94108
TELEPHONE (41s) 969-6600
tr,,-, *rrrr*
6437.02
January 10, 1990
Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
Chief, Technical Assistance & Analysis Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807
Re3 Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice
Dear Jeanne:
Recently, I telephoned your office to ask your opinion as to
whether a proposed initiative 
submitted to the Attorney General
and given an official 
summary date, but never printed or
circulated for signature purposes, would trigger a 21-day post
petition turn-in campaign statement pursuant to Government Code
584200.5(f). You indicated that no campaign filing would be
reguired based upon the following set of facts.
Our client, Gary J. Flynn, submitted a proposed initiative
to the Attorney General on May 26, l-989. The Attorney General
identified the proposed initiative 
as #SA-89RF001-3. The Attorney
General provided a title 
and summary of the initiative 
and
designated the official 
sunmary date as July 18, L989. Prior to
July 18, L989, our client wished to make technical amendments to
the proposed initiative. 
These tecirnical ainendments had not been
submitted to the Attorney General prior to the Attorney General
finalizing title 
and summary on July L8, L989. In our
conversations with the Attorney Generalrs office, it was their
recommendation that even though the amendments were technical in
nature, they would treat the amended initiative 
as a new
initiative 
and start the title 
and sunmary period over again.
The Attorney Generalrs office, however, did agree to expedite
title 
and summary since they recognized these amendments to be
technical. 
On JuIy 31, 1989, w€ submitted to the Attorney
Generalts office the revised initiative. 
On August 2, l-989, the
Attorney General provided title 
and summary for the initiative
and designated August 2, L989 as the official 
sunmary date. This
initiative 
vras numbered by the Attorney General as SA-89R80018.
u,
L!} 
aF.
i-i
Y 
:.J
F...
*4
.-O
,2




Jeanne Pritchard
January 10, 1990
Page 2
The amended initiative 
was printed and cj.rculated to voters
for signature. on December 5, 1989, the first petitions of the
initiative 
were turned in to several county clerks. 
Pursuant to
Government code 584200.5(f) the committee, Citizens for
Legislative Ethics, supported by individuals and Republican
organizations, which had been formed to support the guatification
of the initiative, 
was reguired to file within 21 days of
December 5, 1989. The committee did make a public disclosure on
FPPC fol:ro 42o on December 26, L9a9, covering the period January 1
through December 19, 1989.
For the first initiative 
that was never circulated, the
Attorney General had established December 15, 1989 as the last
day for proponents to file with the county clerks. 
Since this
date was subsequent to the date petitions were first submitted on
the circulated initiative, 
we questioned whether an additional
filing would be required by our committee, calculating 2l- days
after December L5, 1989? we also told you that this conmittee
had neither raised or spent any money during these 1,0 days.
You advised that since the arnendments r+ere technical in
nature and since the first initiative 
was never printed or
circulated, it would not be necessary to fiLe a campaign
statement as required by Government Code 584200.5(f) for the
first initiative 
nurnbered sA-89RF0013.
If I have rnisstated any of the facts of our conversation or
my understanding of your advice to us, please notify me at your
earliest convenience. If this letter is an accurate
nenorialization of our conversation, please sign this letter
below and return it to me for our records. If you have any
questions, please do not hesitate to contact rne.
